target.name: Sakana AI
target.founder: David Ha, Llion Jones, Ren Ito

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $12.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

data.narrative:
Sakana AI is a seed-stage AI & Software company founded by David Ha, Llion Jones, and Ren Ito, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money with $230M+ total raised. The peer universe consists of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), including Thinking Machines Lab, Liquid AI, Field AI, and Twelve Labs across seed and Series A stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate the assessment. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $2.5B. Peer-median methodology across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range spanning $250M to $12.0B, reflecting wide dispersion in early-stage AI valuations. The peer-median approach is weighted appropriately given the limited but relevant comp set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.8B to $2.9B, with a median of $2.3B. This implies modest upside from the current $1.0B+ valuation. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of capital intensity in foundational AI development, near-term revenue trajectory relative to burn rate, and continued access to high-compute resources. The wide peer range ($250M–$12.0B) reflects material uncertainty in how seed-stage AI investors are currently pricing differentiation and technical capability across the sector.
